speaker: GRIFFITH
text: How does your Grace?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: O Griffith, sick to death.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My legs like loaden branches bow to th’ earth,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Willing to leave their burden. Reach a chair.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: So. Now, methinks, I feel a little ease.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Didst thou not tell me, Griffith, as thou ledst me,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: That the great child of honor, Cardinal Wolsey,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Was dead?

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Yes, madam, but I think your Grace,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Out of the pain you suffered, gave no ear to ’t.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Prithee, good Griffith, tell me how he died.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: If well, he stepped before me happily

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For my example.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Well, the voice goes, madam;

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: For after the stout Earl Northumberland

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Arrested him at York and brought him forward,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: As a man sorely tainted, to his answer,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: He fell sick suddenly and grew so ill

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: He could not sit his mule.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Alas, poor man!

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: At last, with easy roads, he came to Leicester,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Lodged in the abbey, where the reverend abbot

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: With all his convent honorably received him;

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: To whom he gave these words: “O Father Abbot,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: An old man, broken with the storms of state,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Is come to lay his weary bones among you.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Give him a little earth, for charity.”

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: So went to bed, where eagerly his sickness

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Pursued him still; and three nights after this,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: About the hour of eight, which he himself

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Foretold should be his last, full of repentance,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Continual meditations, tears, and sorrows,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: He gave his honors to the world again,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: His blessèd part to heaven, and slept in peace.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: So may he rest. His faults lie gently on him!

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yet thus far, Griffith, give me leave to speak him,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And yet with charity. He was a man

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Of an unbounded stomach, ever ranking

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Himself with princes; one that by suggestion

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Tied all the kingdom. Simony was fair play.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: His own opinion was his law. I’ th’ presence

speaker: KATHERINE
text: He would say untruths, and be ever double

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Both in his words and meaning. He was never,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But where he meant to ruin, pitiful.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: His promises were, as he then was, mighty,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But his performance, as he is now, nothing.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Of his own body he was ill, and gave

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The clergy ill example.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Noble madam,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Men’s evil manners live in brass; their virtues

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: We write in water. May it please your Highness

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: To hear me speak his good now?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yes, good Griffith;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I were malicious else.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: This cardinal,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Though from an humble stock, undoubtedly

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Was fashioned to much honor. From his cradle

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one:

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Exceeding wise, fair-spoken, and persuading;

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Lofty and sour to them that loved him not,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: But, to those men that sought him, sweet as summer.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: And though he were unsatisfied in getting,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Which was a sin, yet in bestowing, madam,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: He was most princely. Ever witness for him

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Those twins of learning that he raised in you,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Ipswich and Oxford, one of which fell with him,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Unwilling to outlive the good that did it;

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: The other, though unfinished, yet so famous,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: So excellent in art, and still so rising,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: That Christendom shall ever speak his virtue.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: His overthrow heaped happiness upon him,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: For then, and not till then, he felt himself,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: And found the blessedness of being little.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: And, to add greater honors to his age

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Than man could give him, he died fearing God.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: After my death I wish no other herald,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No other speaker of my living actions,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To keep mine honor from corruption

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But such an honest chronicler as Griffith.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Whom I most hated living, thou hast made me,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: With thy religious truth and modesty,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Now in his ashes honor. Peace be with him!—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Patience, be near me still, and set me lower.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I have not long to trouble thee.—Good Griffith,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Cause the musicians play me that sad note

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I named my knell, whilst I sit meditating

speaker: KATHERINE
text: On that celestial harmony I go to.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: She is asleep. Good wench, let’s sit down quiet,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: For fear we wake her. Softly, gentle Patience.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Spirits of peace, where are you? Are you all gone,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And leave me here in wretchedness behind you?

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Madam, we are here.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: It is not you I call for.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Saw you none enter since I slept?

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: None, madam.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No? Saw you not, even now, a blessed troop

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Invite me to a banquet, whose bright faces

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Cast thousand beams upon me, like the sun?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: They promised me eternal happiness

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And brought me garlands, Griffith, which I feel

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I am not worthy yet to wear. I shall, assuredly.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: I am most joyful, madam, such good dreams

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Possess your fancy.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Bid the music leave.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: They are harsh and heavy to me.

speaker: PATIENCE
text: Do you note

speaker: PATIENCE
text: How much her Grace is altered on the sudden?

speaker: PATIENCE
text: How long her face is drawn? How pale she looks,

speaker: PATIENCE
text: And of an earthy cold? Mark her eyes.

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: She is going, wench. Pray, pray.

speaker: PATIENCE
text: Heaven comfort her!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: An ’t like your Grace—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You are a saucy fellow.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Deserve we no more reverence?

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: You are to blame,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: Knowing she will not lose her wonted greatness,

speaker: GRIFFITH
text: To use so rude behavior. Go to. Kneel.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I humbly do entreat your Highness’ pardon.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My haste made me unmannerly. There is staying

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A gentleman sent from the King to see you.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Admit him entrance, Griffith.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But this fellow

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Let me ne’er see again.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: If my sight fail not,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You should be Lord Ambassador from the Emperor,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My royal nephew, and your name Capuchius.

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Madam, the same. Your servant.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: O my lord,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The times and titles now are altered strangely

speaker: KATHERINE
text: With me since first you knew me. But I pray you,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: What is your pleasure with me?

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Noble lady,

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: First, mine own service to your Grace; the next,

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: The King’s request that I would visit you,

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Who grieves much for your weakness, and by me

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Sends you his princely commendations,

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: And heartily entreats you take good comfort.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: O, my good lord, that comfort comes too late;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: ’Tis like a pardon after execution.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: That gentle physic given in time had cured me.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But now I am past all comforts here but prayers.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: How does his Highness?

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Madam, in good health.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: So may he ever do, and ever flourish,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: When I shall dwell with worms, and my poor name

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Banished the kingdom.—Patience, is that letter

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I caused you write yet sent away?

speaker: PATIENCE
text: No, madam.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Sir, I most humbly pray you to deliver

speaker: KATHERINE
text: This to my lord the King—

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Most willing, madam.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: In which I have commended to his goodness

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The model of our chaste loves, his young daughter—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The dews of heaven fall thick in blessings on her!—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Beseeching him to give her virtuous breeding—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: She is young and of a noble, modest nature;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I hope she will deserve well—and a little

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To love her for her mother’s sake that loved him,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Heaven knows how dearly. My next poor petition

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Is that his noble Grace would have some pity

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Upon my wretched women, that so long

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Have followed both my fortunes faithfully,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Of which there is not one, I dare avow—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And now I should not lie—but will deserve,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For virtue and true beauty of the soul,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For honesty and decent carriage,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A right good husband. Let him be a noble;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And sure those men are happy that shall have ’em.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The last is for my men—they are the poorest,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But poverty could never draw ’em from me—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: That they may have their wages duly paid ’em,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And something over to remember me by.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: If heaven had pleased to have given me longer life

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And able means, we had not parted thus.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: These are the whole contents. And, good my lord,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: By that you love the dearest in this world,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: As you wish Christian peace to souls departed,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Stand these poor people’s friend, and urge the King

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To do me this last right.

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: By heaven, I will,

speaker: CAPUCHIUS
text: Or let me lose the fashion of a man!

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I thank you, honest lord. Remember me

speaker: KATHERINE
text: In all humility unto his Highness.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Say his long trouble now is passing

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Out of this world. Tell him in death I blessed him,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For so I will. Mine eyes grow dim. Farewell,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My lord.—Griffith, farewell.—Nay, Patience,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You must not leave me yet. I must to bed;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Call in more women. When I am dead, good wench,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Let me be used with honor. Strew me over

speaker: KATHERINE
text: With maiden flowers, that all the world may know

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I was a chaste wife to my grave. Embalm me,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Then lay me forth. Although unqueened, yet like

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A queen and daughter to a king inter me.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I can no more.
